points.text: Hitler's invasion of the Soviet Union in 1941 opened a two-front war, crucially engaging the USSR against Nazi Germany.
The Eastern Front was a massive battleground where four-fifths of all Wehrmacht soldiers who died in the war perished fighting the Red Army.
The Soviet Union suffered immense casualties, with 27 million USSR citizens dying in what they call 'the Great Patriotic War.'
The Red Army's victory at Stalingrad marked a turning point, leading to the systematic annihilation of the German Sixth Army.
The Red Army's advance into East Prussia contributed to the encirclement and eventual defeat of Nazi Germany.
